subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Detailed table of contents and numbered subsections enhance navigability and demonstrate strong organizational skills.
Concise, informative title and clearly sign-posted headings guide the reader effectively.
Introduction, body and conclusion are all present and identifiable, showing a coherent investigative flow.
weaknesses: Minor digressions into background theory (e.g., extended Maclaurin series discussion) interrupt the logical progression.
Some repetition when describing each candidate equation could be tightened for conciseness.
Bibliography formatting is inconsistent, mixing styles and italics.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed organization at band 4, coherence at band 3 and relevance at band 3, indicating a well-structured report with minor lapses. The clear structure and signposting merit a high score, but occasional digressions and repetition prevent the top band. A score of 3 of 4 accurately reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Variables, parameters and constants are almost always defined and notation is used consistently (e.g. T(t), k, C, MAE).
Effective use of tables, scatter plots, trend lines and residual plots helps the reader follow the modelling argument.
Logical ordering of mathematical arguments allows reproducibility of steps.
weaknesses: Occasional notational slips, such as reusing 'x' inconsistently and introducing two constants 'c' in integration.
Some graphs lack tick marks, scale labels and clear units, reducing precision.
Uncertainty estimates and error bars are omitted in data-collection discussions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands were assessed at band 3 (âmostly consistentâ language, âeffectiveâ representations, âgoodâ structure), indicating solid mathematical communication with room for improved rigor and clarity. A score of 3 of 4 suitably reflects these attributes.

strengths: Rationale is engaging and personal, clearly showing why the student chose this topic.
Collecting personal data and choosing safe-temperature bounds demonstrate significant independent thinking.
Exploring three different models and computing MAE shows a good level of initiative.
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques applied follow standard textbook methods rather than novel approaches.
Personal approach, while clear, remains fairly conventional for a Newton-cooling investigation.
Creativity is present but ideas are sensible extensions rather than highly original innovations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand of personal engagement was placed in band 2, reflecting significant but not outstanding independence, personal approach and creativity. A score of 2 of 3 aligns with these assessments.

strengths: Thoughtful comparison of MAE values and discussion of residual plots show meaningful reflection.
Evaluation of model strengths, limitations and possible extensions (e.g. cup material, seasonal repeats).
Reflection appears at key stages and the conclusion acknowledges practical implications and limits.
weaknesses: Statistical justification for MAE thresholds and error-bound discussions is limited.
Evaluation remains fairly general without quantified future-implication analysis.
Critical analysis of model deviations could be deeper (e.g. systematic residual patterns, convection/evaporation effects).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All reflection strands were assessed at band 2 (âthoughtful analysisâ, âclear evaluationâ, âmeaningful reflectionâ), indicating a solid reflective component that lacks sustained depth. A score of 2 of 3 appropriately represents this performance.

strengths: Wide-ranging use of relevant mathematics: differential equations, integration, exponential and cubic regression, Maclaurin series and MAE.
Accurate derivations of the cooling model and clear parameter estimation supported by residual plots.
The final model closely matches the data, demonstrating comprehensive application of mathematics to the aim.
weaknesses: Minor algebraic/transcription errors in series manipulation and coefficient matching.
Limited justification for truncating the Maclaurin series; error bounds are not estimated.
Notation around Newtonâs Law constants is ambiguous and key regression statistics (e.g. RÂ²) are omitted.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands were assessed at bands 5 (relevance), 4 (level appropriateness) and 4 (understanding and accuracy). The exploration applies mathematics comprehensively and competently, though minor rigour issues prevent the top band. A score of 5 of 6 reflects this strong but not flawless performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: Mathematical arguments are ordered logically and workings are readable.  Occasional notational clutter (e.g., unnecessary âÃ xÂ²â in the Maclaurin step) slightly obscures clarity, but overall the communication is good and the reader can reproduce steps without difficulty.

Derivations are mostly correct and the final model closely matches the data (supported by MAE and residual plot).  Minor algebraic slips and unclear series manipulation introduce some inaccuracies, so understanding is good and mostly correct rather than âthorough and completely correctâ.

The exploration employs tables, scatter plots with trend lines, residual plots and algebraic working.  These are well-chosen and help the reader follow the argument.  Axis labels and units are generally present, though some diagrams exported from Desmos lack scale markings, so the use of multiple representations is effective rather than outstanding.

The report follows a clear investigative flow: introduction â data â model development â analysis â conclusion.  Transitions between these stages are mostly smooth and each step is justified, showing logical progression from raw data to the final model.  Minor digressions (e.g., lengthy background on Maclaurin series and repeated description of Newtonâs Law) occasionally interrupt the line of argument, so coherence is good rather than exemplary.

Trying three modelling strategies, applying Maclaurin expansion and computing MAE show a good level of initiative and some creativity.  The ideas are sensible extensions rather than highly original innovations, so the work fits the âgoodâ band.

There is thoughtful comparison of MAE values, discussion of residual plots and critique of the Maclaurin approximation.  Analysis is sound but not particularly deep; for instance, statistical justification of the MAE threshold is limited.  Hence the strand sits in the âthoughtfulâ rather than âinsightfulâ band.

The student evaluates strengths, limitations and possible improvements, and proposes extensions (different cup materials, seasonal repeats).  These comments show clear evaluation of outcomes, yet they remain fairly general and do not include quantified impact, so they align with band 2.

The student reflects on data-collection errors, model limitations and practical implications for drinking coffee.  Reflection appears at key stages (after each model and in the evaluation), demonstrating meaningful engagement, though depth is not sustained throughout the whole exploration.

Variables, parameters and constants are almost always defined and appropriate notation (e.g.  T(t), k, C, MAE) is used consistently.  A few slipsâsuch as treating âxâ as both a time variable and a dummy in the Maclaurin expansionâprevent full accuracy, keeping the strand in the âmostly consistentâ band.

The task arises from the studentâs personal experience and is pursued in a manner that reflects individual interest (e.g., chosen temperature limits, cup type).  This provides a clear personal stamp, yet the overall approach remains fairly conventional for a Newton-cooling IA.

Most material directly supports the modelling task, but the essay exceeds what is needed (19 pages plus long derivations copied verbatim).  Some repetition occurs when each of the three candidate equations is described.  Overall relevance is high, yet the writing could be tightened for conciseness.

Differential equations, integration, exponential regression, cubic regression, Maclaurin series and MAE are all relevant to modelling cooling.  Mathematics is wide-ranging and applied to address the aim comprehensively, though not at the absolute highest sophistication level.

Collecting personal data, deciding on safe-temperature bounds and iterating through three different models all show notable independent thinking, but the mathematical techniques follow standard textbook methods.  The strand therefore demonstrates significant, though not outstanding, independence.

The student provides a detailed table of contents, numbered subsections and clearly sign-posted headings, allowing the reader to locate information quickly.  Graphs, tables and appendices are referenced correctly from the body text.  Introduction, body and conclusion are all present and identifiable, showing a very strong sense of structure.

Most techniques (first-order differential equation, regressions, series expansion) are at or slightly above SL level and are handled competently.  Small gaps in rigourâsuch as limited justification for truncating the seriesâprevent a mark of 5 or 6.

annotations.comment: The description of Newtonâs Law uses â-kâ ambiguously; clarify that k is positive and the negative sign arises from the proportionality, to ensure accurate understanding of constants.
The table of contents is detailed and correctly references all sections, enhancing navigability and showing strong organizational skills.
The rationale is engaging and personal, clearly showing why the student chose this topic and demonstrating significant independent thinking.
Graph 1.0âs axes lack tick marks and unit labels on both axes; add scales and labels to ensure the representation is precise and reproducible.
In the integration steps, two constants âcâ are introduced; combine them into a single constant when integrating to maintain mathematical rigor.
When discussing residuals, identify and interpret any systematic deviations to demonstrate depth of critical analysis of your chosen model.
The title is concise, informative and immediately conveys the IAâs focus, guiding the reader effectively into the exploration.
Assuming only radiative heat loss oversimplifies coffee cooling; consider convection and evaporation or discuss their relative impact to deepen your evaluation.
The data-collection method lacks uncertainty estimates and error bars; include measurement error and discuss its potential effect on the model.
Table 1.0 is clear but would benefit from explicit column headers for units (Â°C, min) and a note on rounding to reinforce clarity.
Solving for C and k with only the first two data points risks bias; applying exponential regression to all data would give a more robust estimate of k.
Linearizing the decay and plotting ln|TâTa| is an effective representation, reinforcing the modelâs exponential nature through multiple forms.
The regression line is given but RÂ² or correlation coefficient is omitted; include these statistics to quantify the strength of the linear fit.
The choice of a cubic regression is plausible but needs a rationale (e.g., parsimony vs. overfitting) to justify its selection over other degrees.
In the Maclaurin expansion for e^(âkt) you switch to âxâ; maintain consistency by using ââktâ throughout to avoid confusion.
Neglecting higher-order terms in the Maclaurin series requires justification; estimate the remainder term to show the error bound for truncation.
When matching coefficients in the cubic approximation, check the algebraic manipulation of tÂ² and tÂ³ terms to correct minor transcription errors.
The MAE calculation shows good comparison but omit the explicit summation; display the sum â|xiâx| before dividing to clarify your working.
The residual plot for Equation 2 is well presented and shows no pattern, supporting the conclusion that this model fits the data effectively.
The derivation of tâ and tâ is accurate and clearly addresses your aim, demonstrating purposeful use of the final model.
The conclusion reflects on strengths and acknowledges limitations (mug material, room conditions), showing thoughtful evaluation of outcomes.
The bibliography mixes formatting styles and italics; apply a single citation style consistently for presentation coherence.
The health threshold selection of 60 Â°C is reasonable but could be strengthened by discussing the underlying epidemiological evidence more fully.
Using a moving average trend line in Excel visualizes smoothing, but an exponential trend-line would better match Newtonâs Law of Cooling for parameter estimation.
The horizontal line y = 27 shown as room temperature should be labeled âTââ in the legend and annotated on the graph for clarity.
